caption: The image depicts a group of people engaged in the traditional Korean communal activity of Kimjang, characterized by their use of aprons, gloves, and the preparation of salted napa cabbage mixed with red chili paste on large tables.

The image depicts a person wearing an apron and black gloves, actively handling salted napa cabbage and red chili paste (gochugaru) to prepare kimchi, a traditional Korean dish, in a communal setting known as Kimjang.

The image visually characterizes the Korean independence movement with individuals dressed in white hanbok, waving the Taegeukgi (Korean flag), and participating in what appears to be a celebratory event marking the 104th anniversary of the independence movement.

The image visually represents figures from the Korean independence movement, characterized by individuals wearing traditional white hanbok and period clothing, with one person holding a document and another waving the Taegeukgi, symbolizing Korean national pride and resistance against colonial rule.

The image showcases a woman adorned in a vibrant red Jeontonghonrye, a traditional Korean wedding attire, featuring intricate floral embroidery and a decorative headpiece, standing in front of a wooden ritual table with symbolic items like mandarin ducks and candles.

In this image of a Jeontonghonrye, a Korean traditional wedding, a couple is dressed in vibrant ceremonial hanbok, seated before a ritual table adorned with wooden mandarin ducks and candles, symbolizing prosperity and good fortune.

The image depicts an ancestral rite (Jesa) setup, featuring a ritual table adorned with various food offerings such as fruits, nuts, and cakes, with calligraphy panels in the background, symbolizing respect for ancestors.

The image depicts a Korean ancestral rite (Jesa) with a ritual table adorned with neatly arranged food offerings and family members performing deep bows, including individuals dressed in traditional hanbok attire.
The image depicts a group of individuals participating in a Seongmyo ceremony, standing in a line on a paved area with a statue and trees in the background, symbolizing respect and remembrance.

In this image, a group of individuals dressed in formal attire is engaged in a Seongmyo ceremony at a Korean cemetery, characterized by their respectful posture and the presence of food offerings placed before the grave.
The Cheomseongdae observatory is visually characterized by its cylindrical stacked-stone tower with a square top frame, illuminated in pink light, standing in an open field under a dark night sky.
Cheomseongdae, a Korean stone observatory, is visually characterized by its cylindrical stacked-stone tower with a square top frame, standing in an open field under a clear sky.

Gyeongbokgung, a Korean royal palace, is visually characterized by its wooden pavilions with upturned eaves, vibrant dancheong paintwork, and elevated stone platforms, all illuminated against the deep blue twilight sky.

Gyeongbokgung, a Korean royal palace, is visually characterized by its wooden pavilions with upturned eaves, vibrant dancheong paintwork, and elevated stone platforms, creating a harmonious blend of traditional Korean architecture and natural beauty.

Cheonggyecheon, a restored urban stream in Seoul, is visually characterized by a shallow waterway bordered by stone embankments and pedestrian walkways, with autumn foliage adding a vibrant touch to the scene.

The image showcases a statue of Admiral Yi Sun-shin, a Korean naval hero, dressed in Joseon military armor and holding a sword, standing prominently in a plaza adorned with fountains and surrounded by modern urban architecture.

Jeol and Sebae are performing a Korean formal deep bow, characterized by kneeling and bowing deeply with hands on the floor, often wearing hanbok, in front of an altar adorned with flowers and a framed portrait.

In this image, two individuals are performing Jeol and Sebae, kneeling and bowing deeply with hands on the floor, while dressed in traditional hanbok, indicating a respectful and formal gesture within Korean culture.

In the image, a family is engaged in a traditional Korean Jeol and Sebae ritual, characterized by individuals wearing hanbok, with one person kneeling and bowing deeply with hands on the floor, while others sit or stand nearby, creating a respectful and familial atmosphere.

The image shows a statue of Sejongdaewang, King Sejong the Great, seated in royal Joseon robes with a winged crown, accompanied by Hangul script and a group of individuals in traditional attire standing in front of it.

The statue of Sejongdaewang, King Sejong the Great, is depicted as a seated figure in royal Joseon robes with a winged crown, and the name "세종대왕" is inscribed on the pedestal, accompanied by Hangul script.

The image depicts a person flying a Yeon-nalligi, a traditional Korean kite, characterized by its vibrant rainbow-colored tail and shield-like shape, against a backdrop of a clear sky and a grassy park.
The image depicts a person engaged in Yeon-nalligi, the traditional Korean kite flying activity, characterized by a colorful kite soaring against a clear sky.

In this image, a group of people is seated on the floor around a colorful floral-patterned cloth, engaging in a traditional Korean activity that resembles Yutnori, a game involving wooden sticks and a game board with circular stations.

The children are dressed in traditional Korean hanbok while playing Yutnori, a Korean traditional board game, as evidenced by the four wooden yut sticks mid-air and the game board with circular stations on the floor.

The image showcases a group of women performing Ganggangsullae, a traditional Korean circle dance, dressed in hanbok and holding hands in a large circle formation outdoors with a scenic bridge in the background.
The image depicts a group of women performing Ganggangsullae, a traditional Korean circle dance, dressed in hanbok and holding hands in a circular formation under a full moon.
The image showcases a performer in a Talchum costume, characterized by an exaggerated painted mask and vibrant, flowing robes, embodying the essence of traditional Korean masked dance-drama.
The image showcases a variety of Talchum masks with exaggerated facial expressions and vibrant colors, typical of Korean masked dance-drama performances.
The image shows a bowl of Ramyeon, featuring yellow curly noodles in an orange-red spicy broth, garnished with various toppings and served with chopsticks on a wooden table.

The image shows a pot of Ramyeon, Korean instant noodles with yellow curly noodles in an orange-red spicy broth, accompanied by a container of kimchi, a traditional Korean side dish made from fermented vegetables.

The image showcases gimbap, Korean seaweed rice rolls, characterized by their cylindrical shape, colorful fillings including shrimp, cucumber, carrots, and sesame seeds, all wrapped in dark-green seaweed and sliced into round pieces.
The image showcases a plate of Wanja-jeon, Korean pan-fried meatballs, characterized by their small, round, golden-brown patties coated with a light egg batter, garnished with green onions.

The image showcases Hangwa, traditional Korean confections, characterized by their vibrant pastel colors and intricate pressed floral or geometric patterns, adorned with small decorative pieces such as pine nuts and dried fruits.

The image showcases a box of Hangwa, traditional Korean confections, featuring an array of pastel-colored rectangular pieces with pressed floral and geometric patterns, arranged in a grid-like pattern.

The image showcases a bowl of Yaksik, a traditional Korean sweet rice dish, featuring a glossy mound of sticky glutinous rice mixed with chestnuts, jujubes, and pine nuts, presented on a checkered cloth atop a bamboo mat.

The image showcases a plate of yakgwa, a traditional Korean honey cookie, featuring small, flower-shaped pastries with a glossy, dark golden-brown coating, garnished with delicate edible flowers and green leaves.

The dish in the image appears to be a variation of Bulgogi, featuring thinly sliced beef and vegetables simmered in a rich broth, with visible onions and green onions, and a glossy sauce that suggests a savory, flavorful preparation typical of Korean cuisine.

The image showcases a bowl of Tteokguk and Mandu soup, a traditional Korean New Year dish, featuring a clear broth with white oval sliced rice cakes (tteok) and crescent-shaped dumplings (mandu), garnished with chopped green onions, red chili pepper, and a sprinkle of seaweed.

The image showcases a bowl of Tteokguk and Mandu soup, a traditional Korean New Year dish, featuring a clear broth with white oval sliced rice cakes (tteok) and crescent-shaped dumplings (mandu), garnished with a sprig of seaweed and a slice of green onion.
The image showcases Japchae, a Korean dish featuring translucent brown glass noodles mixed with vibrant julienned vegetables and garnished with sesame seeds, all coated in a glossy sesame oil sheen.
The image showcases a traditional Korean pavilion with ornate roof tiles and colorful decorations, situated atop a stone wall, reflecting the architectural style of Namhansanseong fortress.

The image showcases Haenggung, a Korean temporary royal palace, characterized by its wooden buildings with curved tile roofs and dancheong-painted eaves arranged around courtyards, surrounded by lush greenery and traditional stone walls.

The image showcases the Paldalmun Gate, a gate of Hwaseong Fortress, characterized by its arched stone gateway topped with a wooden pavilion and flanked by curved fortress walls, adorned with traditional Korean architectural details and red flags.

The EXPO Hanbit Tower, a prominent Korean landmark, is visually characterized by its tall white observation tower with a disc-shaped deck and pointed spire, set against a backdrop of lush green hills under an overcast sky.

The image visually captures the essence of the Korean War (1950-1953) through its depiction of soldiers in military attire, a military vehicle, and a rural setting, reflecting the historical context of the conflict.
The image depicts a scene of post-war devastation, with dilapidated structures and overgrown vegetation, characteristic of the aftermath of the Korean War (1950-1953).

The image depicts a traditional Korean village scene, likely from before the Korean War, showcasing thatched-roof houses and people engaged in daily activities near a body of water, without any direct visual elements characteristic of the Korean War era.
The family is engaged in the traditional Korean shuttlecock-kicking game, Jegichagi, on a sunny day surrounded by blooming cherry blossoms and lush greenery.

The image depicts a child dressed in traditional Korean attire, including a pink jeogori and gray pants, kicking a small paper-and-coin jegi into the air, embodying the playful spirit of the Jegichagi game.
